text: Science has a clearer story about methane hydrates than social media does. Methane hydrates are icy structures where methane gas is trapped in water, found deep under the ocean and in permafrost. As climate change warms our planet, these hydrates may destabilize. What happens then? Scientists warn that releasing methane could accelerate global warming—it's 25 times more potent than CO2. But we still need research. Uncertainty remains about how much methane could be released and its effects. Want to know more about our planet? Hit like and follow for more science insights!
